sowing.method: Planting of grafted saplings (e.g. stone grafting/inarching) in prepared pits

seed_rate.notes: 100-120 grafted saplings per hectare under standard 10m x 10m spacing layout.

sowing.soil_conditions: Deep, well-drained, red sandy loam or alluvial soils with pH 5.5-7.5.

irrigation.notes: Irrigate young saplings every 3-4 days in dry weather.
Support active stem growth. Stop irrigation 2-3 months before flowering to induce dormancy.
Critical moisture phase. Water stress causes fruit drop and small sizes.

fertilizer.notes: Apply mixed with well-decomposed manure after post-harvest cleaning.

fertilizer.method: Soil surface application in tree basin drip line
fertilizer.notes: Potassium helps in fruit sizing, flavor development, and quality.

maturity_indicators: Fruit shoulders align with stem or become raised, ground color changes to light green/yellow
Sap flow from pedicel decreases and skin develops a waxy bloom
harvest.harvest_method: Hand harvesting with pedicel snaps or using mango poles
harvest.notes: Keep 1-2 cm stalk intact during harvest to prevent latex burn on skin. Store at 12-14°C.
